purpose: Evaluate model and workflow choices for retail pricing and promotion decisions where margin, vendor funding, competitive response, cannibalization, and inventory constraints jointly determine whether a promotion is economically justified.
design_principles: Reward margin-aware decisions over unit-lift chasing.
Include competitor actions without making share loss certain.
Test pull-forward, cannibalization, supply, and vendor funding together.
Require a concrete price, depth, or hold decision.
Escalate only when downside or ambiguity exceeds routine promotion scope.

ambiguity_sources: competitor price move without share proof
attachment or basket effect offsets margin loss
escalation_patterns: customer trust or price image risk

forbidden_behaviors: optimize unit lift without margin math
assert competitor share loss without evidence

criteria: Accounts for vendor funding or cost changes.
Respects margin floor or markdown budget.
Separates incremental demand from pull-forward or cannibalization.
Treats competitor action as uncertain unless evidence is provided.
Uses inventory cover, replenishment timing, or channel constraints.
Avoids promotional actions that create stockout or service risk.
Provides a concrete price, depth, hold, or targeted promotion decision.
Defines guardrails, duration, or next read.
States appropriate confidence and escalation stance.

items.title: Coffee Price Match Under Supply Constraint

input.facts: Competitor A is at $7.99 for 5 days on 12-count coffee. Our price is $9.49, cost $5.75, margin floor 32 percent. Top 80 stores have 1.4 weeks cover and replenishment arrives in 12 days. Last loyalty promo lifted units 24 percent but post-promo demand fell 11 percent.

items.question: Decide whether to price match, target, or hold. Include margin and supply reasoning.

expected_output.recommended_price_or_depth: hold or target a limited loyalty offer that stays above margin floor
expected_output.margin_read: $7.99 creates about 28 percent margin, below 32 percent floor
risk_controls: monitor competitor in-stock and share data

escalation_triggers: competitor in-stock data confirms severe share loss
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer chases unit lift while missing margin, funding, supply, or pull-forward risk.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward margin-aware economics, demand interpretation, risk controls, and human-review boundaries.

items.title: Vendor-Funded Beauty Gift Promo

input.facts: Vendor funds $3 per unit for a $29 serum gift set for one week. Unit cost is $12. Regular margin is 58.6 percent. Proposed promo price is $24. Inventory cover is 5.5 weeks, ecommerce cover 2.1 weeks. Promo excludes marketplace channel.

items.question: Recommend whether to run the promo and where.

expected_output.recommended_price_or_depth: $24 for one week in stores and owned ecommerce, excluding marketplace
expected_output.margin_read: vendor funding keeps effective margin acceptable at about 62.5 percent on funded units

items.expected_failure_mode: The answer chases unit lift while missing margin, funding, supply, or pull-forward risk.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward margin-aware economics, demand interpretation, risk controls, and human-review boundaries.

items.title: Patio Markdown Before Rainy Weekend

input.facts: Patio sets are 64 percent sold through versus 72 percent plan. Weather forecast shows two rainy weekends in the Southeast, outdoor traffic down 18-25 percent. Markdown budget allows 15 percent now or 25 percent after Memorial Day. Current gross margin at 15 percent off is 43 percent; at 25 percent off is 35 percent.

items.question: Recommend markdown timing and depth.

expected_output.decision: take a targeted 15 percent markdown now in weather-impacted regions
expected_output.margin_read: 15 percent preserves 43 percent margin versus 35 percent at 25 percent
risk_controls: avoid national markdown unless sell-through worsens

must_not_include: hold all markdown until after Memorial Day
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer chases unit lift while missing margin, funding, supply, or pull-forward risk.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward margin-aware economics, demand interpretation, risk controls, and human-review boundaries.

items.title: Accessory Attachment Promo Tradeoff

input.facts: Tablet case retail $39.99, cost $13.50. Proposed 20 percent off when bought with a tablet. Attachment rate is 22 percent, target 30 percent. Tablet margin is unaffected. Case inventory cover is 7 weeks. Prior standalone 20 percent promo lifted case units but lowered attachment quality.

items.question: Recommend whether to use the attachment promo.

expected_output.recommended_price_or_depth: 20 percent off case only with tablet purchase
expected_output.margin_read: case margin remains positive and cover is high
risk_controls: measure attachment rate and basket margin

items.expected_failure_mode: The answer chases unit lift while missing margin, funding, supply, or pull-forward risk.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward margin-aware economics, demand interpretation, risk controls, and human-review boundaries.

items.title: Berry Weather Spike With DC Capacity

input.source_note: Heatwave forecast adds 18 percent berry demand in the Southwest for 6 days. The DC has 92 percent cold-chain capacity already committed, inbound raspberries are 2 days late, and last year's comp was stockout-censored in 38 percent of stores.

items.question: Decide whether to price, promote, hold, or target. Include margin, demand, and risk-control reasoning.

expected_output.decision: do not approve a blanket promotion without passing economics and supply controls
expected_output.recommended_price_or_depth: hold, target, or cap the offer until deterministic controls clear
expected_output.margin_read: validate margin floor, funding, MAP, and cost exposure before any price move
expected_output.demand_read: treat demand lift as uncertain when stockouts, trend spikes, or competitor actions contaminate the read

must_not_include: treat last year's comp as clean demand
escalation_triggers: cold-chain capacity exceeds safe receiving limit
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer chases unit lift while missing margin, funding, supply, or pull-forward risk.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward margin-aware economics, demand interpretation, risk controls, and human-review boundaries.
